title: Grünbaum's Problem on Edge Density of 4-Critical Planar Graphs
statement_text: Determine the precise value of $L := \limsup |E(G)|/|V(G)|$ over all $4$-critical planar graphs $G$.
context_text: Grünbaum defined $L := \limsup |E(G)|/|V(G)|$ over all $4$-critical planar graphs $G$, proved $L \geq 79/39$, and asked for the precise value. The problem is motivated by pre-processing algorithms for $3$-coloring planar graphs: upper bounds on $H(4,n)$ yield polynomial-time edge-reduction steps before applying more expensive algorithms.
paper_contribution: Proves $L = 2.5$ by constructing, for each sufficiently large integer $m$, a $4$-critical planar graph $P_m$ on at most $m$ vertices with $\lim_{m\to\infty} |E(P_m)|/m = 2.5$, matching the upper bound $L \leq 2.5$ previously established by Koester (1991).
paper_title: Solution to a problem of Grünbaum on the edge density of $4$-critical planar graphs

title: Yao–Zhou Conjecture on Edge Density of 4-Critical Planar Graphs
context_text: Yao and Zhou established the lower bound $L \geq 7/3$ and conjectured this to be the exact value. Their bound improved earlier constructions of Grünbaum and Koester, and the conjecture represented the state of the art on the lower-bound side before the present paper.
notes: Reference [13] is truncated in the supplied PDF text; publication year cannot be determined from the available content.
paper_title: Solution to a problem of Grünbaum on the edge density of $4$-critical planar graphs
